devNotes.text: Since this icon does not convey any meaningful information, we can hide this icon from assistive technologies using the aria-hidden="true" attribute.
Because this link opens in a new tab, we will want to add screen reader only text to the end of the link content to convey this expectation to screen reader users, e.g. (opens in a new tab)
Ensure sufficient color contrast between foreground text and its background, meeting a minimum ratio of 4.5:1 for normal text and 3:1 for large text.
This interactive element must be reachable and operable via keyboard. Ensure it receives focus and can be activated with Enter or Space.
Provide a visible focus indicator for this element so keyboard users can see where focus is at all times.
This image requires a descriptive alt attribute that conveys its meaning or function to screen reader users.
Ensure this form input has a programmatically associated label using a <label> element or aria-label attribute.
This error message should be announced to screen reader users by associating it with the input field using aria-describedby.
Use a native <button> element here rather than a <div> or <span>, so that it is keyboard-operable and announced correctly by assistive technologies.
This modal dialog should trap focus within it while open, and return focus to the triggering element when dismissed.

appDevNotes.text: This icon does not convey meaningful information and should be hidden from assistive technologies. On iOS use accessibilityHidden = true; on Android set importantForAccessibility="no".
Ensure this interactive element meets the minimum touch target size of 44×44pt (iOS) or 48×48dp (Android) to support users with motor impairments.
Provide a meaningful accessibility label for this element. On iOS use accessibilityLabel; on Android use android:contentDescription.
Ensure sufficient color contrast between foreground and background, meeting a minimum ratio of 4.5:1 for normal text and 3:1 for large text, across both light and dark mode.
This screen should have a descriptive and unique title announced when it comes into focus, so screen reader users understand where they are in the app.
The reading and focus order of this screen should follow the visual layout. On iOS, set accessibilityElements in the correct order; on Android use traversalBefore/traversalAfter.
When this action triggers a status change or notification, the result should be announced via an accessibility announcement. Use UIAccessibility.post on iOS or AccessibilityEvent on Android.
This image conveys meaningful content and requires a descriptive content description so it is not skipped by screen readers.
This custom component should expose its role and state to assistive technologies. Use accessibilityTraits/accessibilityRole on iOS or accessibilityDelegate on Android.
Verify that this experience is fully operable using Switch Control (iOS) and Switch Access (Android), not just touch or voice.
